name: One Thirteen
description_short: One Thirteen Credit Union is a Colorado Springs-based credit union offering checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services to local members.
description_long:
One Thirteen Credit Union operates as a member-owned financial cooperative serving the Colorado Springs, Colorado community. The institution provides traditional credit union services designed to enhance financial well-being through competitive rates and member-focused benefits. According to NCUA data presented on their service page, the credit union maintains a modest but active membership base with 1,064 savings accounts, 493 checking accounts totaling $2.26M in deposits, and 300 active credit cardholders.

One Thirteen offers a comprehensive suite of retail banking products including high-yield checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, share certificates (CDs), and tax-advantaged retirement accounts (IRA/KEOGH). On the lending side, they provide flexible auto loans and mortgage options. Their digital banking capabilities include mobile banking apps, online loan payments, e-statements, and online banking platforms. Members can access funds through ATM networks and physical branch locations in the Colorado Springs area.

As a credit union, One Thirteen distinguishes itself through the cooperative model, which typically offers lower fees and competitive rates compared to traditional banks. The institution emphasizes member benefits, exclusive discounts, and personalized financial planning services. They provide dedicated financial planning and wealth management support alongside standard banking functions. The credit union maintains NCUA insurance protection on member accounts.

One Thirteen is best suited for Colorado Springs residents seeking traditional banking services with a community-focused, member-owned institution. However, the website content is limited in detail regarding specific rates, fees, loan terms, and full product specifications. The institution appears to be a smaller, regional credit union rather than a major financial player, which may limit product availability or geographic accessibility for non-local members.
services: Checking accounts (Share Draft accounts) with free checks and no monthly fees
Regular share savings accounts with competitive interest rates
Money market accounts with higher interest rates and flexible access
IRA and KEOGH retirement accounts with tax-advantaged savings
Flexible auto loans with customized terms
Mortgage options for home purchase and refinancing
Mobile banking app for account management on-the-go
Online banking platform with bill pay and fund transfers
pros: Credit union membership model typically offers lower fees and more competitive rates than traditional banks
NCUA-insured accounts provide federal protection on member deposits
Mobile banking app allows account management, transfers, bill pay, and balance checks on smartphones
Free checking accounts (Share Draft) with no monthly fees and free checks available
Tax-advantaged retirement account options (IRA/KEOGH) for long-term savings
Share certificates (CDs) with low minimum balances for higher interest earnings
Flexible auto loans and mortgage options tailored to member needs
cons: Limited to Colorado Springs area with no indication of national or online-only membership options
Website provides no specific information on current interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules for comparison
Small institution with only 493 checking accounts and 1,064 savings accounts suggests limited resources and product development compared to larger banks
Credit card offerings appear minimal (only 300 cardholders) with incomplete product details on the website
No mention of investment services, brokerage accounts, or advanced wealth management tools
best_for: Colorado Springs residents seeking a community-focused credit union with lower fees
Consumers prioritizing member-owned institutions over commercial bank models
Local savers looking for NCUA-insured accounts with competitive rates
Members needing basic checking, savings, auto loans, and mortgages in the Colorado Springs area
diagnosis: One Thirteen Credit Union is best for Colorado Springs residents seeking traditional banking services with a member-owned, community-focused institution that emphasizes lower fees and competitive rates. The main caveat is geographic limitation to the Colorado Springs area and lack of detailed product specifications, rates, and fees on their service page, which makes comparison shopping difficult for prospective members.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
